mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which forces equity markets to price in lower corporate revenues and yields negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and consumer price indices indicate strong demand pulling through to corporate top lines without immediate supply constraints, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >60% of occurrences, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals improving manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying in equity markets and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations exceed 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 20-day forward returns, or the predictive IC drops below 0.01 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A housing starts figure falling below 1.5 million units indicates a sharp contraction in construction demand and consumer confidence, which precedes broader economic slowdown and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive when HOUST drops below 1.5 million, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: High federal funds rates combined with elevated consumer price indices create stagflationary pressure that compresses equity valuations and reduces risk appetite, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined high-rate-high-inflation regime yields positive forward returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in predictive models.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth signals robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings expansion, which drives sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: GDPC1 acceleration fails to predict positive 60-day returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal becomes negative when credit spreads widen beyond 150 basis points.
